description: Add a task spec generation flow to Agent Board so users can create an implementation-ready Agent Spec for an existing task directly from the VS Code extension UI. The generated spec must populate the task JSON fields `description`, `acceptanceCriteria`, `qaChecklist`, `designQaChecklist`, `validationCommands`, and `constraints` using project context from `.agent-board/project.json` and the selected task data. This task should not call external AI APIs; implement a deterministic local generator that produces useful defaults and preserves all unknown task fields.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can trigger Agent Spec generation for a selected task from the board UI.
The generator reads current project context from `.agent-board/project.json` and current task data from `.agent-board/tasks/TASK-ID.json`.
The task JSON is updated with non-empty `description`, `acceptanceCriteria`, `qaChecklist`, `designQaChecklist`, `validationCommands`, and `constraints` fields.
Existing unknown fields in the task JSON are preserved exactly when the task file is updated.
Existing meaningful values are not overwritten unless the user explicitly confirms regeneration or the fields are empty.
Generated validation commands include project-level validation commands, with `npm run compile` included for this repository.
The webview refreshes after generation so the updated task spec is visible without restarting VS Code.
Generation failures show a clear VS Code error message and do not partially corrupt the task JSON.
qaChecklist: Create or select a task with empty spec fields and run the new spec generation action.
Verify `.agent-board/tasks/TASK-002.json` contains populated implementation-ready spec fields after generation.
Verify unrelated task fields such as `id`, `priority`, `assignedAgent`, `qaAgent`, `relevantFiles`, custom fields, and activity history are preserved.
Verify the board UI updates to show the generated description and checklists after generation.
Verify attempting to regenerate a task with existing spec content requires confirmation or leaves existing content unchanged.
Verify invalid or missing `.agent-board/project.json` produces a clear error and does not modify the task file.
Verify invalid task JSON produces a clear error and does not write a broken replacement file.
designQaChecklist: The generation control is easy to find on the selected task detail view or task action area.
The action label is concise and operational, such as `Generate Spec`.
The UI does not introduce a marketing-style layout or oversized explanatory content.
Loading, success, and error states are visible and do not shift the board layout awkwardly.
If confirmation is required for regeneration, the prompt clearly states that existing spec fields may be replaced.
constraints: Do not add real Claude, Codex, OpenAI, or other external AI API calls.
Keep extension backend logic in `src/` and React webview logic in `webview/`.
Follow existing TypeScript and React patterns in this repository.
Preserve unknown fields in all Agent Board JSON files.
Before writing a task file, reread the latest file contents to avoid overwriting newer changes.
Use `.agent-board/project.json` as the source of project context and the selected `.agent-board/tasks/TASK-ID.json` as the source of task context.
Keep the generated spec deterministic and repo-local for this task.
